title: "Help reading kernel logs"

### Post by mistcloud-misty on 2015-12-29
My laptop crashed randomly this morning and this is what I found as part of the problem. My question is, I don't really understand much of it of the report, and was hoping someone could help me understand what happened. 

I run Ubuntu 14.04 with kernel 3.13 (only one that works with my wireless). The long 00/00 section was all highlighted red.

### Post by buzzingrobot on 2015-12-29
> ata1.00: failed command: IDENTIFY DEVICE

That and the other messages, to my eye, point to an issue with a drive.  Might be something temporary that will pass, or something serious.  I don't know enough about hardware to offer suggestions.

OTHER THAN... Backup anything you don't want to lose in case the drive is beginning to fail.

---

### Post by mistcloud-misty on 2015-12-29
I did check the SMART data on the disc and it passed all the tests and said it was healthy. It is a new computer (3 months old), and the error has not recurred after the crash so far. 

I will still do the backup just in case while I figure out if anything is wrong.

---
